definition: The Fed faces simultaneous Type I (premature easing from OER masking) and Type II (insufficient tightening from lagging indicators) policy error risk. These errors may be positively correlated: OER masking encourages premature easing (Type I), which simultaneously constitutes insufficient tightening (Type II) by definition.
evidence: Each error type individually well-documented in monetary policy literature
Compound probability may be elevated due to positive correlation between error types
Historical sample insufficient to estimate compound probability directly
open_questions: What is the compound probability of both errors occurring simultaneously?
Is the positive correlation between Type I and Type II errors a structural feature or coincidence?
What monitoring signals distinguish genuine disinflation from OER masking?
